w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: (Open in Microsoft Word and press Ctrl+A then F9 to update the table of contents.)

FlipTrack is a full-featured reseller dashboard that helps you track inventory, record sales, manage expenses, analyze profits, and crosslist items across platforms. It works on any device as a Progressive Web App (PWA) with offline support and cloud sync.
Open fliptrack.live in any modern browser (Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge). On mobile, tap the browser menu and select “Add to Home Screen” to install FlipTrack as a standalone app.
Click the sync indicator (colored dot) in the top-right header to open the Account Menu.
Select “Sign Up” and enter your email and password.
Once signed in, your data automatically syncs to the cloud across all your devices.
You can use FlipTrack without an account — all data is stored locally on your device.
When you first open FlipTrack, an interactive guided tour walks you through all 25 features across 10 sections. You can restart the tour anytime by clicking the “? Tour” button on the Dashboard.

FlipTrack includes sample inventory, sales, and expenses so you can explore the app before adding your own data. To remove demo data, open the Account Menu and tap “Clear Demo Data.” This only removes items with the FT- prefix and [demo] tags — your real data is preserved.
Try the demo data first to see how all the features work together before adding your own inventory.

FlipTrack has 15 views accessible from the header navigation bar (desktop) or the bottom navigation bar (mobile). On mobile, tap “More” to see additional views like Shipping, Sourcing, Tax Center, and Buyers. Navigation groups collapse into expandable sections on desktop for a cleaner layout.
FlipTrack offers three subscription tiers to match your reselling needs:
— Core tools to get started: inventory (up to 50 items), sales logging, expense tracking, dashboard, CSV export, and supplies management.

— Everything in Free plus: Insights & Profit Dashboard, Value Breakdown & Reports, Crosslisting (Copy & List, templates), AI features (Identify, Listing, Price Research), Sourcing & Haul tracking, Buyer CRM, and Shipping view.
— Everything in Pro plus: eBay/Etsy API integrations, Whatnot show planning, Batch Scan & Batch List, Tax Center, Repricing rules, Photo tools, and PDF report exports.

Features above your current tier appear in the navigation with a lock icon. Clicking a locked feature shows an upgrade prompt with tier comparison details. Free users see a soft-cap warning when they reach 50 inventory items.
The Dashboard is your command center — a real-time overview of your reselling business. Everything updates automatically as you add items and record sales.
Six metric cards across the top show your key performance indicators:
Total value of all items at list price. Click to see the Value Breakdown.
Sum of all recorded sales. Shows total sale count below.
Revenue minus cost, fees, shipping, and expenses. Shows margin percentage.
Return on investment: profit divided by total cost basis.
Count of bulk items below their stock alert threshold. Single-quantity items excluded.
Average number of days from listing to first sale.

Set targets for revenue, profit, units sold, and sell-through rate. Progress bars track your progress throughout the month with color coding: green when on track, yellow when behind, red when significantly under target.
Click the pencil icon next to the Goals panel to edit your targets.

A visual calendar where each day is color-coded by profit: green days are profitable, red days show losses, and gray days have no activity. This helps you identify your best selling days and spot trends at a glance.
FlipTrack automatically identifies items that may need a price adjustment based on how long they have been listed without a sale:
Click “Apply” next to any suggestion to update the price instantly.

The Death Pile widget highlights items sitting unsold for extended periods. Items are flagged by urgency level with colored indicators. Click “Fix” on any item to open its drawer for repricing or relisting. Items that have appeared in 3 or more Whatnot shows without selling are also flagged with a Whatnot show badge.
A chart showing your revenue split by selling platform (eBay, Poshmark, Mercari, Amazon, Whatnot, etc.) so you can see which marketplaces perform best for your business.
Quick-view panels show your 6 most recently added inventory items and 8 latest sales, giving you instant access to recent activity without switching views.
One-click buttons for exporting inventory CSV, sales CSV, tax CSV, P&L PDF reports, and tax summary PDFs — all accessible directly from the Dashboard.

The Profit Dashboard is a dedicated view accessible from the Profit tab in the navigation bar. It provides a deep-dive financial analysis of every item in your inventory with interactive filtering and drill-down capabilities.
— Four KPI cards at the top: Total Profit, Average Margin, Best Margin Item, and Total Revenue. Each updates in real-time as you filter.

— Every inventory item listed with cost, list price, estimated fees, shipping, profit per unit, and margin percentage. Margins are color-coded: green for high (20%+), blue for mid (10–19%), and red for low (under 10%).
— Filter the profit table by selling platform to see profitability per marketplace.
— Click any item row to open the item drawer for editing. The “Go to Sales” button jumps directly to the Sales view.
FlipTrack provides three ways to add items: manual entry, AI photo identification, and batch barcode scanning.
Click the “+ Add Item” button in the header (desktop) or the floating action button (mobile) to open the Add Item modal.
(required) — the item title as you would list it
— scan or type the barcode number
— select from the dropdown (Clothing, Electronics, Books, Home, Toys, Sports, etc.)
— appears based on the selected category
— what you paid for the item
— how many you have in stock
— NWT, NWOT, EUC, Good, Fair, Poor
— select one or more selling platforms
— estimated platform fees and shipping cost

Upload up to 5 photos per item. You can drag and drop images, use the camera, or select from your gallery. Each photo can be cropped with adjustable aspect ratios before saving. Images larger than 15 MB are automatically rejected with a friendly error message. All photo slots include descriptive alt text for screen reader accessibility.
FlipTrack includes built-in photo enhancement tools to help your listings stand out:
— Automatically removes the background from product photos, leaving a clean white background.
— One-click brightness, contrast, and color correction that analyzes the image and automatically adjusts for optimal quality.
— Adjustable aspect ratio cropping with preset ratios for common listing formats (1:1 square for eBay/Poshmark, 4:3 for Mercari, etc.).
When the category is set to Books, additional fields appear: ISBN, Author, Publisher, Edition, Printing Year, Signed status, and Sales Rank. Enter an ISBN to auto-lookup book details.

When eBay is selected as a platform, additional eBay-specific fields appear in a dedicated “eBay Listing Details” section on the Details tab: Brand, Color, Size, Material, MPN, Model, Style, Pattern, and eBay Description. These fields map directly to eBay Item Specifics and improve search visibility.

The Dimensions tab lets you enter package weight (lb/oz or kg/g) and dimensions (L × W × H). Dimensional weight is automatically calculated. FlipTrack recommends the best packaging options based on your item’s measurements and automatically converts metric values to imperial for eBay listings.
Click “Repeat Last” to pre-fill the form with your previous item’s details. Great for adding similar items in bulk.

Click the camera icon (“Identify”) in the header to use FlipTrack’s AI vision feature. Take a photo or select an image — the AI analyzes the item and returns name, brand, category, subcategory, estimated condition, and suggested price with a confidence badge. Click “Add to Inventory” to pre-fill the add form.

Click the lightning bolt icon (“Batch”) in the header to open rapid scanning mode. Your camera detects barcodes in real-time (UPC, EAN, Code 128, Code 39, ITF). Each scan adds items to a batch queue. Review all scanned items, then click “Add All to Inventory” to import them at once.
Batch scanning is perfect for thrift hauls — scan dozens of items in minutes, review the batch, and add them all with one tap.
The Inventory view is your central hub for browsing, filtering, editing, and managing all of your items.

Each row displays: photo thumbnail, item name with SKU and category tags, platform badges (color-coded), stock quantity, cost, selling price, margin percentage, days listed, and action buttons. Click any row to open the full item drawer for editing.
Use the search bar to find items by name, SKU, or category. The filter panel provides advanced filtering:
— Select one or more platforms to show only items listed there.
— Filter by category (dynamically populated from your data).
— Appears when a single category is selected.
— Filter by NWT, NWOT, EUC, Good, Fair, etc.
— Filter by smoke-free, smoke exposure, or unset.
— Find items listed for a specific duration.
Filters stack — apply multiple simultaneously. An active filter count badge shows how many are applied. Click “Clear Filters” to reset.
Sort your inventory by clicking column headers or using the sort dropdown: Newest/Oldest First, Name A–Z, Best Margin, Highest/Lowest Profit, Longest/Newest Listed, Low Stock First, Price High–Low.
Stock alerts only apply to bulk items (quantity greater than one). Single-quantity items never trigger low stock or out-of-stock alerts, keeping your notifications clean.
Select items using checkboxes or “Toggle All.” A bulk action bar appears with options:
— Record sales for multiple items at once.
— Generate barcode labels for selected items.
Click the “More ▾” dropdown for advanced bulk operations:
— Increase or decrease prices by percentage or fixed amount with live preview.
— Assign a category to all selected items.
— Toggle platforms with Unlisted mutual exclusivity enforcement.
— Export only selected items as a CSV file.

Click any item row to open the side drawer with full editing capabilities. All fields from the Add Item form are available, plus listing status per platform, quick platform toggles, eBay/Etsy auto-push, inline price editing with margin preview, and photo management. Changes save automatically when you close the drawer.
Free tier users see a warning banner when they reach 50 inventory items, encouraging an upgrade for unlimited inventory. Adding items is not blocked — the banner is informational.
Grab the drag handle on any row to reorder items manually. Your custom sort order persists across sessions.

Deleted items go to the Trash rather than being permanently removed. Click the Trash icon in the toolbar to view deleted items. From the Trash modal, you can restore individual items or empty the trash permanently.
The Sales Log tracks every sale with detailed financial data for accurate profit tracking.
Click “+ Record a Sale” in the Sales view
Click “Mark as Sold” on any inventory item
Use bulk actions to mark multiple items as sold
— Select from your inventory (auto-shows cost, list price, and expected profit)
— Toggle between “per unit” and “total for quantity”
— Platform fees (auto-estimated based on platform, e.g., eBay ~12.9%, Poshmark 20%)
— Your shipping cost for this order
— Which marketplace the sale occurred on
— Optional — link to an existing buyer or auto-create a new one
The sales log displays date, item name, platform badge, quantity, sale amount, unit cost, fees + shipping, and net profit. Profit is color-coded green for positive and red for negative.

Filter by date range (From/To date pickers) or search by item name, buyer, or platform. Running totals update dynamically — the summary bar shows count, total revenue, and total profit for only the currently filtered sales.
Use date range filters to see monthly or quarterly performance at a glance.
Track every business expense for accurate profit calculations and tax reporting.
— When the expense occurred (defaults to today)
— Shipping Supplies, Packaging Materials, Platform Fees, Sourcing, Storage, Printing, Software, Marketing, Returns, Office Supplies, or Other
Expenses are included in the P&L report and tax calculations automatically.
Log every business expense — shipping supplies, subscriptions, mileage, and packaging all reduce your taxable income.
Keep track of your packing and shipping supplies so you never run out during a busy shipping day.
— Supply name (e.g., “6x9 Poly Mailer”, “USPS Medium Flat Rate Box”)
— Boxes, Mailers, Bubble Wrap, Tape, Labels, Envelopes, Tissue, or Other

Each supply row shows name, category, current quantity (with +/− buttons for quick adjustment), unit cost, alert threshold, notes, and a delete button. Quantities below the alert threshold display a red “LOW” badge.
The Insights view provides in-depth analytics to help you understand your business performance and make data-driven decisions.
Five key metrics: Total Revenue, Net Profit, Units Sold, Sell-Through Rate, and Average Margin.
— 5 items with the best return on investment
— Items with the most sales in the last 30 days
— Items listed 30+ days with zero sales
Top 5 categories ranked by profit with revenue, units sold, total profit, and margin percentage. Top 6 platforms ranked by revenue with units sold and listing count per platform.
Top expense categories by amount, with 30-day and year-to-date totals.
Sell-through rate, inventory turnover rate, and average days to sell — metrics that help you understand how quickly your inventory moves.
— View sales volume and revenue over time to spot seasonal patterns.
— Compare sales performance across platforms side-by-side.
— Track how your average margins change over time.
Generate professional reports for your records, accountant, or tax filing.
Toggle between Weekly and Monthly views. Use arrow buttons to navigate periods.
A complete P&L for the selected period: Revenue, COGS + Platform Fees, Gross Profit, Operating Expenses (by category), and Net Profit (highlighted with color coding).

Two tables breaking down profit by category and platform. Each row shows sales count, revenue, COGS, fees, profit, and margin percentage. Margins are color-coded: green 20%+, yellow 10–19%, red under 10%.
Sub-period breakdowns showing how performance trended over the selected period.
Click “P&L Report PDF” or “Tax Summary PDF” to generate formatted PDFs suitable for printing or sending to your accountant.
Understand where your capital is tied up with a visual breakdown of inventory value by category and subcategory.

Summary cards show Total Inventory Value, Total Cost Basis, Total Units, and Average Margin. Each category displays as a collapsible row with value, cost, unit count, and margin. Click any category or subcategory to jump to the Inventory view pre-filtered to that segment.
Manage your listings across multiple selling platforms from a single dashboard.

Connect your eBay and Etsy accounts via OAuth for direct integration. Connected accounts show username/shop name and last sync timestamp. eBay and Etsy API calls now have a 15-second timeout with user-friendly error messages (“Request timed out — try again” or “Network error — check your internet connection”). Mobile OAuth connections use redirect flow instead of popup for reliability. Failed auto-list and crosslist cache operations now show toast notifications instead of failing silently.
FlipTrack provides deep eBay integration through the eBay Inventory API, enabling you to create, publish, update, and end eBay listings directly from FlipTrack.
— One-click process: Inventory Item Creation, Category Detection via Taxonomy API, Business Policies auto-detection, Offer creation and publishing.
— FlipTrack maps its condition tags to eBay’s ConditionEnum values (NWT → NEW, EUC → USED_VERY_GOOD, etc.).
— Auto-generated branded description with specifications table, condition notes, and shipping banner.
— Edits auto-push to eBay and re-publish immediately.
— Click Sync or wait for automatic 5-minute intervals. Matches by SKU or eBay item ID.
— Fetches recent orders, auto-records sales, and optionally delists from other platforms.
— One-click withdrawal from eBay via the listing matrix.
Fill in as many eBay Listing Details fields as possible before publishing. eBay rewards complete listings with better search placement.
Dedicated Whatnot panel for managing live selling shows. All features work offline using your local data — no account connection needed. See Section 21 for the full guide.
Six quick metrics: Active Listings, Expiring Soon, Expired, Sold Elsewhere, Not Listed, and Single-Platform items.

The Overview tab shows expiring and expired listings with Relist/Delist buttons. The Listing Matrix shows every item against every platform with color-coded status cells. The Templates tab lets you create reusable listing templates by category.
Manage your shipping workflow from sale to delivery.
— All unshipped orders in a sortable, filterable queue.
— Mark shipped with carrier, tracking number, and shipping cost.
— Generate printable slips with your logo for individual or batch orders.
— Log returns with date, reason, refund amount, and notes.
— Mark multiple orders shipped, print batch slips, or export shipping log as CSV.
Track where you find your inventory and measure ROI of sourcing trips.
— Date, location, total spent, mileage, and notes.
— Connect inventory items to specific hauls for per-haul ROI tracking.
— Total Hauls, Total Invested, Average Spend Per Haul, Average Items Per Haul.
— Leaderboard ranking your sourcing locations by ROI.
— Linked items, total cost, revenue from sold items, ROI percentage, and cost per item.
FlipTrack automates tax tracking for self-employed resellers, including quarterly estimated payments and Schedule C preparation.
View each quarter’s financial summary with due dates: Q1 (Jan–Mar, due Apr 15), Q2 (Apr–Jun, due Jun 15), Q3 (Jul–Sep, due Sep 15), Q4 (Oct–Dec, due Jan 15 next year).

Each quarter displays: Revenue, COGS, Platform Fees, Shipping Costs, Gross Profit, Operating Expenses, Net Profit, Estimated Income Tax, Self-Employment Tax (15.3%), and Quarterly Estimated Tax Payment.
Track business mileage for tax deductions. Add entries with date, miles, and purpose. FlipTrack calculates your total deduction using the current IRS standard mileage rate ($0.67/mile for 2025).
Export Tax Summary PDFs, raw transaction CSVs, and Schedule C summaries. Year-over-year comparison tracks business growth.
Build relationships with your buyers by tracking purchase history and preferences.
— Usernames on eBay, Poshmark, Mercari, etc.
FlipTrack automatically assigns tiers based on total spending:
Click any buyer to see their full purchase history with dates, items, platforms, and amounts.
Research pricing before listing to maximize profit margins.
— Enter any UPC or EAN to look up product details, historical pricing, and market offers.
— Search by product name or keywords to find matching products with images, brands, and price ranges.
— Direct links to comparable listings on eBay, Amazon, Poshmark, Depop, Mercari, Grailed, StockX, GOAT, and Etsy.
Check eBay sold listings first — they show what items actually sell for, not just what sellers are asking.
Move data in and out of FlipTrack with CSV files.
— All items with name, SKU, UPC, category, platforms, quantity, cost, price, margin, notes, and dates.
— All sales with date, item, platform, quantity, sale price, fees, shipping, and profit.
— All expenses with date, category, description, and amount.
— Downloads all three CSVs in sequence.
— Show Prep, Show Results, and All Shows summary exports.

Upload an inventory CSV file (max 5 MB, up to 10,000 rows). FlipTrack auto-detects the delimiter, maps columns intelligently, auto-generates missing SKUs, and supports multiple platforms separated by semicolons or commas.
Export your data regularly as a backup. You can also use exports to migrate data to spreadsheets or accounting software.
Customize FlipTrack to match your preferences.

Toggle between dark and light mode using the sun/moon button in the header. Dark mode uses a deep navy background with cyan accents. Light mode uses a clean white background with blue accents. Your preference is saved automatically.
Click the text size button (Aa) in the header to open the font popover:
: Default (DM Mono) or Easy Read (OpenDyslexic for improved readability)

FlipTrack v1.4 includes comprehensive accessibility improvements: 88 icon-only buttons now have aria-labels, focus trapping in all modals (price research, identify, item drawer), and 44px minimum touch targets on mobile for WCAG compliance.
Click the bell icon to open the Notification Center. FlipTrack sends alerts for:
Low stock warnings (bulk supplies and bulk inventory items below threshold)
— Automatic celebratory notifications when eBay or Etsy syncs detect a new sale
Whatnot show reminders and post-show summaries
Enable push notifications to receive alerts even when the FlipTrack tab is closed. Single-quantity items never generate stock alerts.
Click the sync indicator dot in the header to access your email and account status, manual sync trigger, theme toggle, sign out, and clear demo data.
When signed in, FlipTrack continuously syncs your data to the cloud via Supabase. The sync indicator dot shows status: green (synced), yellow (in progress), red (offline/error). Sync runs

automatically on every save and polls every 30 seconds. Enhanced logout cleanup: signing out now stops all background sync intervals (eBay/Etsy polling), clears IndexedDB metadata, removes localStorage data, and unsubscribes from realtime channels. Toast notifications alert you to any sync interval failures.
FlipTrack works fully offline as a Progressive Web App. Changes are saved locally to IndexedDB and replay automatically when you reconnect.
— Open fliptrack.live in Safari, tap Share, then “Add to Home Screen.”
— Open in Chrome, tap the three-dot menu, then “Install App.”
— Click the install icon in your browser’s address bar.
FlipTrack includes an automated repricing engine that adjusts prices based on rules you define.
— Drop price by a percentage after a set number of days without selling.
— Drop price by 10% after 3+ shows without selling (ships disabled by default).
— Create your own rules with conditions, actions, and target platforms.
FlipTrack provides a comprehensive Whatnot live selling toolkit built entirely on your local inventory data. All features work offline without any account connection.
— Name, date, time, optional notes. Add items from inventory.
— Mark live, then end when finished. Ended shows keep full results.
— Tap sold during a live show. Sale price recorded for analytics.
— Per-item presentation notes for the run sheet.
Clone any past show to create a new one with the same items (minus sold). Template recurring shows for weekly or monthly events.
While live: record peak viewer count and log show expenses (giveaways, promotions). Expenses are deducted from profit calculations.
— Total shows, items shown/sold, revenue, profit, sell-through rate.
— Badges showing highest-performing days and time slots.
— Sparkline of last 10 shows’ sell-through and revenue.
— Bar charts showing sell-through by category.
— Best-selling and hardest-to-move items across all shows.

Uses historical data and inventory to recommend an optimal show lineup: recommended show size, ideal category mix, scored item list (based on margin, show history, category performance, age, freshness), and bulk actions to create a show from selected items.
Three Whatnot-specific exports: Show Prep CSV (numbered checklist), Show Results CSV (sold/unsold with fees and profit), and All Shows CSV (summary table).
The guided tour supports keyboard navigation: Right Arrow or Enter to advance, Left Arrow to go back, Escape to exit.
Shortcuts are disabled while typing in input fields to prevent interference:
— Jump to Inventory view and focus search bar
— Close any open modal, drawer, or overlay
Use “Repeat Last” when adding similar items to save time on data entry.
Set up listing templates in the Crosslist Manager for consistent listings.
Check the Death Pile widget weekly to identify and reprice stale inventory.
Link all sourcing purchases to hauls for per-location ROI tracking.
Use quarterly estimated tax payments from the Tax Center to stay ahead of obligations.
Export CSVs monthly as a data backup.
Enable push notifications for low-stock alerts and listing expirations.
Use Price Research before listing — check eBay sold listings for real market value.
Record all expenses including mileage — every deduction reduces your tax burden.
Review the Insights dashboard weekly for top-performing categories and platforms.
Use the Whatnot Smart Builder before each show for data-driven item selection.
Click the “? Tour” button on the Dashboard at any time to restart the interactive guided tour. For additional support, visit fliptrack.live.
Appendix A: What’s New in Version 1.4
Version 1.4 focuses on security hardening, reliability improvements, and mobile/touch optimization.

— XSS prevention hardened across 19 view and feature files — all dynamic values in HTML attribute contexts (onclick handlers, title attributes, data attributes) now use escAttr() instead of escHtml() for proper attribute-context escaping.
— External API data (eBay/Etsy product titles, images, error messages) now escaped before rendering.
— 15-second timeout on all eBay and Etsy API calls with clear error messages.
— eBay auto-list, crosslist cache generation, and sync interval errors now surface user-visible alerts.
— Comprehensive mutex pattern prevents concurrent sync operations for data integrity.
— All background intervals stopped, IndexedDB metadata cleared, localStorage purged, realtime subscriptions removed.
— All date calculations now use local timezone consistently instead of UTC, preventing off-by-one date errors near midnight.
— All interactive elements now meet WCAG and Apple HIG compliance standards.
— Mobile connections use redirect flow instead of popup for reliable eBay/Etsy authentication.
— Mobile barcode scanner uses photo capture mode instead of video stream.
— Drawer buttons no longer hidden behind iOS home indicator.
— Mobile horizontal overflow eliminated with overflow-x containment.
— 88 icon-only buttons across all views now have descriptive aria-labels.
— Product images and emoji placeholders include meaningful alt text.
— Focus trapping added to price research and identify modals.
— 50+ hardcoded z-index values migrated to CSS custom property scale for consistent layering.
— Font size presets (Small/Default/Large/Extra Large) now scale correctly across all content areas.
— Buttons no longer overlap on mobile screens.
Version 1.3 brings the subscription tier system, comprehensive accessibility improvements, and reliability hardening across the entire application.

— Three tiers (Free, Pro, Unlimited) with hybrid gating. Locked features show a lock icon in the navigation and display an upgrade modal with tier comparison when clicked. Free tier includes a 50-item inventory soft cap with a warning banner.
— Centered overlay with tier comparison cards showing feature breakdowns for Free, Pro ($12/mo), and Unlimited ($25/mo), with “Maybe Later” and “View Pricing” options.
— New profiles table with subscription_tier tracking, auto-created on signup via database trigger, with row-level security.
— All interactive elements (buttons, links, tabs, selects, menu items) now show visible focus outlines via :focus-visible CSS across 6 stylesheets.
— All close buttons, navigation groups, stat cards, and interactive elements now have descriptive aria-labels for screen reader users.
— The upgrade modal uses role=“dialog” with aria-modal and aria-labelledby for proper screen reader announcement.
— All form inputs (bulk action selects, sliders, toggles) are properly associated with <label> elements.
— All photo slots (lightbox, drawer, form) include descriptive alt text for screen readers.
— All photo slot images support Enter key activation with tabindex and role=“button” attributes.
— Expandable nav sections include aria-expanded state for screen reader announcement of open/closed status.
— Concurrent sync operations are now prevented by an _isSyncing flag, eliminating potential data conflicts during rapid saves.
— Authentication event listeners are now initialized once with a guard flag, preventing duplicate listener registration on re-render.
— The notification center prevents duplicate event listener setup when re-initialized.
— The batch barcode scanner now properly clears setTimeout timers when closing, preventing background timer leaks.
— Color utility functions now return a safe fallback (--muted) for unrecognized status codes instead of undefined.
— All CSS custom properties (--hover, --card, --bad) are now defined in :root, eliminating undefined variable references.
— Muted text color updated from #555 to #777 for better readability against the dark background.
Profit Dashboard with per-item profit analysis and margin badges
Sales Analytics & Trends in the Insights view
Advanced Bulk Actions (price adjust, category assign, platform toggle, export CSV)
Sale Notifications for eBay and Etsy syncs
Sales view totals now reflect filtered results
Fixed USPS Small Flat Rate Box dimensions
Keyboard shortcuts no longer fire when typing in form fields
CSV import handles UTF-8 BOM correctly
XSS vulnerability fixes in notification center and profit dashboard
Currency and percentage formatting handles invalid data gracefully
